title: Industry 5.0
subtitle: Inclusive and sustainable manufacturing through human-centred AI and robotics
intro: Industry 5.0 focuses on developing human-centred AI and robotic systems for manufacturing environments, where digital twins, shared autonomy, and collaborative intelligence enhance work performance, safety, and ergonomics.

vision.text: Enable manufacturing ecosystems where humans and intelligent systems collaborate through digital twins, shared autonomy, ergonomic optimization, and cognitive augmentation.

activities.title: Collaborative Digital Twins
activities.text: Designing AI and robotics systems that support workers in production environments through adaptive assistance, digitalization of humans, robots, and environments, ergonomic optimization, shared autonomy, and cognitive augmentation.

activities.title: Multimodal Interaction
activities.text: Developing intuitive multimodal interfaces for human-robot collaboration in manufacturing, integrating gesture recognition and voice commands to improve safety, efficiency, and user experience.

publications.title: TeleSign: A Flexible and Intuitive Gesture-Based Teleoperation Framework
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Advanced Robotics and its Social Impacts (ARSO)

publications.title: A path to industry 5.0 digital twins for human–robot collaboration by bridging NEP+ and ROS
Integrating virtual, mixed, and augmented reality to human–robot interaction applications using game engines: A brief review of accessible software tools and frameworks
Gesture-based robot control: Design challenges and evaluation with humans
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics & Automation (ICRA)
